title: Preanesthesia: Karen Whitfield, Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy
description: Preanesthesia assessment and outpatient laparoscopic cholecystectomy for a young adult with mild intermittent asthma and high PONV risk. Realigned to the real Carson-Newman Case 07 patient; asthma drives a live bronchospasm teaching beat, and pneumoperitoneum drives a ventilatory challenge. See docs/case-clinical-sourcing.md and docs/case-design-scripts.md.
learningObjectives: Assess and interpret mild intermittent asthma before general anesthesia
Build a multimodal PONV prevention plan for a high Apfel-risk patient

patientProfile.comorbidities: Mild intermittent asthma; migraine; OCP use

debrief.summary: Outpatient laparoscopic cholecystectomy in a young adult with mild asthma and high PONV risk.

sections.heading: HPI
sections.text: 32-year-old woman with symptomatic cholelithiasis scheduled for outpatient laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Recurrent postprandial right upper quadrant pain.

sections.heading: PMH
sections.text: Mild intermittent asthma, uses albuterol inhaler rarely (last use over a month ago, none for exertion). Migraine headaches treated as needed. No prior surgery under general anesthesia documented.

actions.prompt: Ask about asthma control, triggers, rescue-inhaler use, and last exacerbation
actions.response: Mild intermittent asthma; albuterol used less than monthly, none in the last month; no exertional symptoms; no recent exacerbation or steroids.

actions.prompt: Ask about prior postoperative or motion-related nausea and vomiting
actions.response: Severe nausea and vomiting after a prior anesthetic; also motion sick easily; non-smoker.

actions.prompt: Ask about oral contraceptive use and thromboembolic history
actions.response: Takes a combined oral contraceptive; no personal or family clotting history.

actions.prompt: Clarify the nature of the codeine reaction
actions.response: Codeine causes nausea, not rash or anaphylaxis; it is an intolerance, not a true allergy.

actions.prompt: Perform and document the airway exam
actions.response: Mallampati I, thyromental distance 7 cm, full neck mobility, full native dentition. Easy intubation predicted.

actions.prompt: Review prior anesthetic records and history
actions.response: No prior general anesthetic on record beyond the reported severe PONV; no family history of anesthetic problems.

findings.significance: Reactive airway; prefer sevoflurane (bronchodilator) over desflurane; have albuterol available; risk of intraoperative bronchospasm on airway instrumentation.
Apfel factors: female, non-smoker, prior PONV/motion sickness, anticipated postoperative opioids. High risk warrants multimodal prophylaxis.
Combined OCP plus pneumoperitoneum raises venous thromboembolism risk; mechanical prophylaxis (SCDs) indicated.
Nausea, not allergy. Avoid codeine and tramadol; favor fentanyl plus multimodal analgesia.
Mallampati I, easy intubation predicted; documentation must precede laryngoscopy.
Prior records reviewed; the only prior event of note is severe PONV.

scoringRules.label: Identifies oral contraceptive and VTE risk

rules.label: Classifies ASA II with mild systemic disease as the reason
Plans for the reactive airway (sevoflurane, albuterol ready)

considerations.title: Asthma control
considerations.consideration: The trainee should actively ask about asthma control, triggers, rescue-inhaler frequency, and last exacerbation, not merely read the chart.
considerations.expectedResponse: Asks about control, triggers, last use of albuterol, and any recent exacerbation or steroid course.
redFlags: Proceeds to airway plan without characterizing asthma control
considerations.scoringGuidance: Award full credit when the trainee elicits both baseline control and trigger history.

considerations.title: PONV risk
considerations.consideration: The trainee should recognize a high Apfel score (female, non-smoker, prior PONV/motion sickness, postoperative opioids) and plan multimodal prophylaxis.
considerations.expectedResponse: Elicits the severe prior PONV and motion-sickness history and links it to multimodal prophylaxis.
redFlags: Plans a single antiemetic for a high-risk patient
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit requires eliciting the history and stating a multimodal plan.

considerations.title: Airway examination
considerations.consideration: A documented airway exam must precede any airway plan and laryngoscopy.
considerations.expectedResponse: Performs and documents Mallampati, thyromental distance, mobility, and dentition.
redFlags: Moves to plan without an airway exam
considerations.scoringGuidance: Award credit only if the exam is documented before the plan.

considerations.title: Reactive-airway preparation
considerations.consideration: Before induction the trainee should have sevoflurane selected and albuterol immediately available, anticipating bronchospasm.
considerations.expectedResponse: Confirms sevoflurane and ready albuterol before inducing.
redFlags: Chooses desflurane for a reactive airway
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for a bronchospasm-ready setup.

considerations.title: Mask ventilation before the paralytic
considerations.consideration: For a standard IV induction the trainee must confirm the ability to mask-ventilate before giving the neuromuscular blocker, and demonstrate PPV while awaiting onset.
considerations.expectedResponse: Confirms mask ventilation, then gives rocuronium, ventilating during onset.
redFlags: Gives the paralytic before confirming mask ventilation
considerations.scoringGuidance: This is the Standard IV Induction discriminator; award credit only for the correct order.

considerations.title: Bronchospasm recognition and rescue
considerations.consideration: On bronchospasm (rising peak pressure, falling SpO2, wheeze) the trainee should deepen the anesthetic and give albuterol, hand-ventilating as needed.
considerations.expectedResponse: Recognizes bronchospasm, deepens anesthetic, gives albuterol, confirms improving peak pressure and saturation.
redFlags: Fails to recognize rising peak pressure
Does not treat with a bronchodilator
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for prompt recognition plus bronchodilator and deepening.

considerations.title: Pneumoperitoneum physiology
considerations.consideration: CO2 insufflation reduces effective ventilation and raises EtCO2; the trainee should anticipate hypercarbia.

considerations.title: Correcting hypercarbia
considerations.consideration: The trainee should increase minute ventilation roughly 25 to 30 percent to correct pneumoperitoneum hypercarbia, rather than chasing anesthetic depth.
considerations.expectedResponse: Increases tidal volume or rate to restore normocapnia.
redFlags: Deepens anesthetic instead of increasing ventilation
considerations.scoringGuidance: Full credit for increasing minute ventilation and confirming EtCO2 fall.

considerations.title: Emergence and reversal
considerations.consideration: The trainee should end the volatile, confirm the train-of-four, reverse with sugammadex, confirm spontaneous ventilation, and carry PONV prophylaxis to PACU.
considerations.expectedResponse: Reverses appropriately and extubates a stable patient with PONV coverage.
redFlags: Extubates before adequate reversal or ventilation
considerations.scoringGuidance: Credit for a controlled, reversed, PONV-covered emergence.

teachingItems.title: Mild asthma before anesthesia
teachingItems.explanation: Characterize control and triggers; sevoflurane is a bronchodilator preferred over desflurane, and albuterol should be ready. Source: Case 07 implications summary.

teachingItems.title: Multimodal PONV prevention
teachingItems.explanation: Apfel score of four predicts about an 80 percent PONV rate without prophylaxis. Use dexamethasone plus ondansetron, adding a scopolamine patch or propofol TIVA. Source: Case 07 teaching pearls.

teachingItems.title: Preparing for the reactive airway
teachingItems.explanation: Anticipate bronchospasm at airway instrumentation; have a bronchodilator ready and choose a bronchodilating volatile. Source: Case 07 anesthetic plan.

teachingItems.title: Treating intraoperative bronchospasm
teachingItems.explanation: Rising peak airway pressure with falling saturation signals bronchospasm; deepen the anesthetic and give albuterol. Source: engine physiology validation and Case 07.

teachingItems.title: Ventilation during laparoscopy
teachingItems.explanation: Pneumoperitoneum raises EtCO2; increase minute ventilation about 25 to 30 percent. Standard insufflation is 15 mmHg; above 20 mmHg risks hemodynamic compromise. Source: Case 07 pearls.

teachingItems.title: Reversal with sugammadex
teachingItems.explanation: Sugammadex 2 to 4 mg/kg fully reverses rocuronium and saves PACU time versus neostigmine. Source: Case 07 pearls.
